summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a jam‑heavy 1991 Greensboro run – crystal‑clear Eyes of the World and Samson jams offset by occasional clipping and glitches.
review: The performance is consistently praised as one of the band’s spring‑tour highlights, with an epic 23‑minute “Eyes of the World,” soaring “Samson,” a teasing Dark Star, and tight renditions of Around & Around, Johnny B. Goode and Playin’ in the Band. The soundboard source delivers generally good fidelity, though listeners note digital noise at the start of FOTD, occasional clipping, a brief skip on Queen Jane, and some over‑modulation later in the set.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈30) are 4–5 stars and focus on stellar musical moments; recording issues are noted but not severe enough to detract from overall enjoyment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear board mix, but exhibits digital noise/clipping at the start of FOTD, a brief skip on Queen Jane, minor static during Samson, a right‑channel tick in Space, and occasional over‑modulation on late‑set numbers (Johnny B. Goode, Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door). Early mix buries Jerry's guitar while Bobby's rhythm and Vince's keyboards sit forward.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds into a marathon “Eyes” that showcases the band’s improvisational depth, while the second set delivers jazzy transitions (Eyes → Playing In The Band), a tight Around & Around, and a rousing Johnny B. Goode finale. Overall pacing is high‑energy with memorable segues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his glowing “eyes” look, excited return of Bruce Hornsby, strong lead work throughout; early mix buries his guitar but he pulls strongly into the Dark Star tease.
band_member_comments.Phil: Only a visual note (a dancer near him); no specific musical comment.
band_member_comments.Bob: His rhythm guitar is prominent in the early mix; a quick lyrical skip on Queen Jane noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards are forward in the mix early on; Bruce Hornsby’s first show back adds fresh energy and melodic texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid, though reviewers mention drummers appearing to “doze off” later and note a tick in the right channel during Space.
